title: furcraea aspera
intro: Furcraea aspera, also known as the rough agave or rough century plant, is a species of succulent plant native to the tropical regions of Central America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant has several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and treating gastrointestinal issues. Furcraea aspera is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and topical creams, and can be found in various traditional medicine systems, including those of the indigenous peoples of Central America.
